description: Performance finding consolidation: N+1 query pattern raised independently by code-reviewer (native) and gemini-review-prompter (external) with different titles but same (file, symbol) bucket. Stage 2 lexical dedup evaluates Jaccard similarity; here the titles diverge enough to remain separate, but the duplicate is expected to be noted by a human judge.

raw_findings.title: N+1 database query in getUsersWithOrders loop
raw_findings.description: getUsersWithOrders iterates over users and fires a separate query per user to fetch orders. Replace with a single JOIN or batch fetch to avoid O(N) database round trips.

raw_findings.title: N+1 database query in getUsersWithOrders loop
raw_findings.description: The loop in getUsersWithOrders causes an N+1 query problem. Each iteration issues a separate SELECT for orders. Use eager loading with a JOIN query instead.

raw_findings.title: getUsersWithOrders loads entire result set without pagination
raw_findings.description: The function fetches all users in one query without a LIMIT clause or cursor-based pagination. On large datasets this causes unbounded memory allocation.

findings.title: N+1 database query in getUsersWithOrders loop
findings.description: getUsersWithOrders iterates over users and fires a separate query per user to fetch orders. Replace with a single JOIN or batch fetch to avoid O(N) database round trips.

findings.title: getUsersWithOrders loads entire result set without pagination
findings.description: The function fetches all users in one query without a LIMIT clause or cursor-based pagination. On large datasets this causes unbounded memory allocation.

consolidated_output.path_and_reason_header: Review path: multi-model (above-threshold diff; reviewers: 2 native + 2 external)

expected_judgement_notes: Consolidation is correct: (1) N+1 finding merged from 2 reviewers (exact finding_id match, Stage 1 dedup); severity unanimous high; raised_by[] populated correctly; (2) pagination finding minority-of-one demoted medium→low (not security, not high-confidence). Attribution is accurate. Descriptions preserve highest-severity description per Stage 2 rules.
